SOURCE_LANGUAGE: Rust
DOC FORMAT: rustdoc (Markdown-based, rendered on docs.rs)

SYNTAX
======
- Item docs (function, struct, enum, trait, method): `///` per line,
  immediately BEFORE the item, NO blank line in between.
- Container docs (modules, crate from inside): `//!` per line, at the
  TOP of the file or right after `mod foo {`.
- Block-comment variant: `/** */` and `/*! */` are allowed, but `///`
  is idiomatic.
- No required '//\* ' prefix - one space after `///`, then Markdown.

STRUCTURE
=========
1. First line: concise one-liner ending with a period. Shown as the
   summary on docs.rs.
2. Blank line.
3. Optional: longer description in one or more Markdown paragraphs.
4. Optional: standardized Markdown sections (in order):
   - `# Errors`        - when returning Result<_, E> or similar
   - `# Panics`        - when the function may panic
   - `# Safety`        - REQUIRED for `unsafe fn`
   - `# Examples`      - with code block(s)

MARKDOWN RULES
==============
- Code identifiers in backticks: `Vec<T>`, `Option::None`.
- Cross-references in brackets to crate items: `[Self::method]`,
  `[crate::module::Type]`. rustdoc resolves them automatically.
- Code blocks with triple backticks. By default code is run as a doctest -
  if not desired, mark with `ignore`, `no_run`, or `text`:
    ```ignore
    not_a_real_function();
    ```
- Inside doctests: `#` at the start of a line hides it from rendering
  but still compiles.

WHAT NOT TO DO
==============
- No @param / @return tags. Parameters are described in prose or via
  Markdown lists with `*`.
- Do not repeat type annotations - the signature already says them.
- `# Arguments` / `# Returns` headers are common in some crates but not
  official - use them only when the project does so consistently.

FUNCTION EXAMPLE
================
/// Calculates the gross total including value-added tax.
///
/// `tax_rate` is expected as a decimal (`0.19` for 19 %). The function
/// requires a non-empty `items` list; otherwise it returns an error.
///
/// # Errors
///
/// Returns [`PricingError::EmptyCart`] when `items` is empty.
///
/// # Examples
///
/// ```
/// # use crate_name::{Item, calculate_total};
/// let items = vec![Item::new(10.0, 2)];
/// let total = calculate_total(&items, 0.19).unwrap();
/// assert!((total - 23.80).abs() < f64::EPSILON);
/// ```
pub fn calculate_total(items: &[Item], tax_rate: f64) -> Result<f64, PricingError> {
    ...
}

UNSAFE EXAMPLE
==============
/// Writes `value` to the address `ptr` without bounds or alignment checks.
///
/// # Safety
///
/// The caller must guarantee that `ptr` points to a valid, writable
/// memory region of at least `size_of::<T>()` bytes and that the
/// alignment is correct.
pub unsafe fn write_unchecked<T>(ptr: *mut T, value: T) { ... }
